whatThisIs: Show the common adult reference ranges for everyday laboratory values - basic chemistry (Na, K, Cl, HCO3, BUN, Cr, Ca, glucose), liver function (AST, ALT, alkaline phosphatase, total bilirubin, albumin), hematology (WBC, hemoglobin, hematocrit, platelets), coagulation (PT/INR, PTT), and the common cardiac and thyroid markers (troponin, BNP, TSH, free T4). Sophie Well presents the canonical range, the units, and a one-line note on the most common reason a value falls outside the range.
whenToUse: Use this when reviewing a paper lab report that does not print the reference range, when a patient brings results from an outside facility that uses different units, or when a learner is interpreting a chart and needs the standard ranges all in one place. Reference ranges are population-derived and lab-specific - the lab printing the report always supersedes a generic table, especially for assays like TSH and troponin where the manufacturer cutoffs and the local reference cohort drive the published interval. Use this as a quick orientation, not a final reference.
